title: Deep Ethnic and Political Divisions Threatening National Cohesion
summary: Sierra Leone's 2023 contested elections exposed deep ethnic and political fault lines, requiring a national unity agreement in 2024 to defuse tensions between the ruling SLPP and the opposition APC. While inflammatory rhetoric and political violence have decreased since the agreement, underlying divisions remain unresolved. The fragility of this arrangement poses ongoing risks to political stability.

title: Executive Overreach and Concentration of Power Undermining Democratic Institutions
summary: Despite constitutional provisions for separation of powers, the executive exercises significant control over state institutions through presidential appointments to key government positions. Public institutions have been turned into vehicles for patronage, and state institutions have reportedly been deployed against political opponents. This concentration of power weakens democratic checks and balances.

title: Suppression of Civil Society and Erosion of Anti-Corruption Accountability
summary: Civil society organizations face incentives through state contracts and board appointments to remain silent on corruption and state injustices, while those critical of the government are labeled as agents of political opposition. The Anti-Corruption Commission collects asset declarations from public officials but keeps their content confidential, limiting public scrutiny. These dynamics undermine accountability and civic space.

title: Political Violence Including State Security Force Abuses and Election-Related Violence
summary: Sierra Leone exhibits four dominant forms of political violence: communal violence, violence by state security forces targeting civilians, riots over national issues, and violence linked to political competition including elections. Demonstrators and opposition parties have faced police violence and restrictions on assembly. These patterns reflect systemic vulnerabilities in the security and political environment.

title: Border Conflict with Guinea Following Military Incursion
summary: In February 2026, Guinean military forces invaded Sierra Leonean territory, attacking border guards with knives and wounding personnel amid an intensifying dispute over control of a border point. This incident marks a significant escalation in bilateral tensions and poses risks to border security and regional stability. The dispute remains unresolved and could further deteriorate.

title: Spillover Risk from Sahel Terrorism and Expanding Militant Networks in Coastal West Africa
summary: The Sahel accounted for 51 percent of global terrorism-related deaths in 2024, and militant groups have expanded financial and logistical networks stretching toward the Gulf of Guinea. Increasing terrorist attacks in Benin and Togo raise alarm about potential spillover into previously unaffected coastal West African countries, including Sierra Leone. Transnational organized crime facilitated by these networks compounds the threat.

title: Treason Charges Against Former President Signaling Politically Motivated Prosecutions
summary: Former President Ernest Bai Koroma was charged with treason in January 2025 for his alleged role in a November 2023 coup attempt, but was released under an ECOWAS-brokered agreement to seek medical treatment abroad. This episode reflects the politicized use of the justice system and heightens tensions between the government and opposition. It also underscores the fragility of political agreements and the role of external mediators.

title: Displacement and Weak Asylum System Capacity
summary: As of June 2025, over 20,500 forcibly displaced persons were registered in Sierra Leone and five neighboring countries under UNHCR's Multi-Country Office. Sierra Leone's asylum system faces significant challenges including resource shortages, limited infrastructure, and weak institutional capacity. The country has also been identified as a transit point for irregular migration toward North Africa and Europe.

title: Barriers to Durable Solutions for Refugees
summary: Many refugees in Sierra Leone remain hesitant to pursue durable solutions such as local integration or voluntary repatriation, creating protracted displacement situations. The government is proposing amendments to the Refugee Protection Act of 2007 to align legislation with international standards and establish a roadmap for sustainable phase-out of refugee programs. Gaps in implementation and resources continue to hinder progress.
